comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
## S8 done — 2026-06-30. docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md + README link.

- AC1: docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md is a complete ordered client walkthrough — 12 sections covering all 8 steps (provision AWS -> deploy Gerrit+review-bot -> project/label/submit-req -> bot/webhooks/events-log -> review->vote pipe (+/rerun) -> replication -> GitHub lock -> verify/operate), each referencing the committed infra/ artifacts + ADRs 0007-0014.
- AC2: NO secrets. Scan over all committed docs/infra/src found only rebar's OWN secret-scanner test fixtures (AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE = AWS public doc example; an RSA-marker test string) in tests/unit/workflow/test_lint.py — fake values, not real, not in the doc/infra. The literal account id 896586841071 does NOT appear in the client doc (placeholder <aws-account-id>). .env / SSH keys / voted.db are gitignored (not tracked).
- AC3: linked from README (Install section, an OPTIONAL/ADVANCED blockquote) — discoverable but explicitly not required for standard rebar use.
- AC4: the secret-handling section (placed first) documents the /rebar/prod/* SSM SecureString -> ephemeral 0600 .env model (ADR-0008/0012) and that no credential is ever committed.

Review: docs-only ticket — self-verified AC coverage + the no-secrets scan (rather than an adversarial code-review subagent, which applies to code stories).

compiled_state.description:
# S8 — Client walkthrough: self-host Gerrit + rebar review-bot on AWS to gate GitHub

## Why
A client wants the same setup we built in d251 — a self-hosted Gerrit server combined
with the rebar review-bot (which imports the rebar review kernel / `rebar.llm`, the
same library the `rebar-mcp` server exposes) — used to GATE commits to a GitHub repo:
GitHub `main` becomes a read-only mirror that only advances via Gerrit after the
deterministic `LLM-Review` vote passes. We need a reproducible, **secret-free**
walkthrough that lets a client replicate this code-review-enforcement topology on
their own AWS account + GitHub repo, distilled from the d251 artifacts (Terraform,
docker-compose, the Gerrit config scripts, and ADRs 0007-0014).

## What
- A new client-facing guide `docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md` that walks through, end to end:
  1. **Prerequisites** — AWS account + creds, a GitHub repo + an `Administration: write`
     token, a domain/DNS zone, Terraform >= 1.10, Anthropic API key.
  2. **Provision the AWS base** (Terraform `infra/`) — EC2 + EBS + EIP + DNS + IAM +
     the SSM SecureString parameter contract (`/rebar/prod/*`) — referencing ADR-0012.
  3. **Deploy Gerrit + the review-bot** (docker-compose behind nginx/TLS) — referencing
     ADR-0007 (receiver-not-MCP-over-HTTP) + ADR-0008 (SSM->.env secrets).
  4. **Configure the Gerrit project + `LLM-Review` label + submit requirement** —
     referencing ADR-0013.
  5. **Wire the review-bot identity + webhooks + events-log** — referencing ADR-0014
     (inbound auth) + ADR-0009 (the verdict->label pipe / `code_review_decision` seam).
  6. **Gerrit -> GitHub replication** (deploy key, non-force) — referencing ADR-0010.
  7. **Lock the GitHub repo** to the replication identity — referencing ADR-0011 + S6-pre.
  8. **Verify + operate** — the e2e check, the manual `/rerun`, the kill-switch, rollback.
  The guide uses the committed `infra/` scripts/config as the source of truth and shows
  the operator commands; it does NOT duplicate large config inline.
- **No secrets in committed docs.** All credentials are PLACEHOLDERS sourced from SSM /
  CI secrets (never literal). The guide explicitly calls out the secret-handling model.
- **Link from the existing install docs.** Add a link to `docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md`
  from the project's install documentation (README / docs index) as an OPTIONAL /
  ADVANCED step titled for "code-review enforcement (self-hosted Gerrit gating GitHub)".

## Scope
In: the `docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md` walkthrough, the link from the install docs, and a
no-secrets verification of all committed documentation. Out: any NEW infrastructure or
code (S8 reuses and references the d251 artifacts produced by S1-S7); per-client
customization beyond the walkthrough.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md` exists and is a complete, ordered client walkthrough
      covering all eight steps above (provision -> deploy -> project/label/submit-req ->
      bot/webhooks/events-log -> replication -> GitHub lock -> verify/operate), each step
      referencing the relevant `infra/` artifact(s) and ADR(s).
- [ ] The new guide and ALL committed documentation in the repo contain NO secrets —
      only placeholders; credentials are sourced from SSM Parameter Store / CI secrets.
      Verified by a secret scan over the committed docs (and `infra/`).
- [ ] `docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md` is linked from the existing install docs (README and/or
      the docs index) as an explicitly OPTIONAL / ADVANCED step for code-review
      enforcement (self-hosted Gerrit gating GitHub) — discoverable from the normal
      install path but not required for standard rebar use.
- [ ] The walkthrough's secret-handling section documents the SSM-SecureString -> ephemeral
      `.env` model (ADR-0008) and that no credential is ever committed.

file_impact.reason: client walkthrough: Gerrit + review-bot on AWS gating GitHub
link the walkthrough as optional/advanced code-review-enforcement step

compiled_state.title: Client walkthrough: self-host Gerrit + rebar review-bot on AWS to gate GitHub
